description: Integrate OpenTelemetry observability into the Flutter app using flutterrific_opentelemetry. This includes: creating a telemetry service abstraction, initializing OpenTelemetry in main.dart, instrumenting the HTTP client (KiroApi) with trace spans, bridging DebugLog to emit OTel log records, adding custom metrics for key operations, and writing unit tests for the new telemetry service.
steps: Step 1 - Create lib/services/telemetry_service.dart: Create an abstract TelemetryService interface and a concrete FlutterOTelTelemetryService implementation that wraps flutterrific_opentelemetry's FlutterOTel. The interface should expose: initialize(), shutdown(), reportError(String source, dynamic error, StackTrace? stackTrace), startSpan(String name, {Map<String, Object>? attributes}), and a getter for the tracer. The concrete implementation calls FlutterOTel.initialize() with serviceName 'kiro-flutter-auth', serviceVersion '1.0.0', tracerName 'kiro-app', and resourceAttributes including deployment.environment and service.namespace. Following the project's abstract interface + DI pattern (like CredentialStore, ConnectivityMonitor).

Step 2 - Create lib/services/telemetry_config.dart: Create a configuration class that reads OpenTelemetry endpoint and settings from compile-time environment variables using String.fromEnvironment(). Support OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT (default: http://localhost:4317), OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL (default: grpc), OTEL_SERVICE_NAME (default: kiro-flutter-auth), OTEL_SERVICE_VERSION (default: 1.0.0). This enables configuring the OTLP backend endpoint at build time via --dart-define flags.

Step 3 - Update lib/main.dart: Import and initialize the telemetry service before runApp. Call FlutterOTel.initialize() early in the runZonedGuarded block (after WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized() but before authManager.initialize()). Replace the existing FlutterError.onError handler to also call FlutterOTel.reportError(). In the runZonedGuarded error handler, also call FlutterOTel.reportError(). Register the telemetry service as a Provider in the MultiProvider so views can access it. Keep the existing DebugLog calls alongside the new OTel calls.

Step 4 - Instrument lib/services/kiro_api.dart with trace spans: Add a TelemetryService parameter to KiroApi's constructor (optional, nullable to avoid breaking existing code). For each API method (listSessions, listAgentTasks, createSession, getSession, generateAgentSessionResponse, listSessionHistory, listConnectionResources), wrap the HTTP call in a span using the tracer: start span before the request with attributes like http.method, http.url, and operation name; set span status to OK on success or Error on failure; record exceptions on the span; end the span in a finally block. Follow the custom tracing pattern from flutterrific_opentelemetry docs.

Step 5 - Bridge DebugLog to OpenTelemetry: Update lib/services/debug_log.dart to optionally forward log entries as OpenTelemetry log records. Add a static setter that accepts a TelemetryService reference. When set, each DebugLog.log() call also creates a brief span or uses the OTel logger to record the log message. This bridges existing debug logging to the OTel backend. Keep the existing in-memory log buffer and debugPrint behavior unchanged.

Step 6 - Update lib/views/app_shell.dart to pass telemetry to KiroApi: When _AuthenticatedBody creates KiroApi, pass the TelemetryService from the Provider context so API calls get instrumented. Use context.read<TelemetryService>() in _AuthenticatedBodyState.initState() to get the telemetry service and pass it to KiroApi.

Step 7 - Write unit tests for TelemetryService: Create test/services/telemetry_service_test.dart. Create a MockTelemetryService that implements TelemetryService (following the project's mock pattern from test/services/auth_manager_init_valid_test.dart). Test that the telemetry service can be instantiated, that reportError does not throw, and that the mock can be injected into KiroApi. Also test the TelemetryConfig class reads default values correctly.

Step 8 - Update .kiro/steering/tech.md: Add flutterrific_opentelemetry and its transitive dependencies (dartastic_opentelemetry, dartastic_opentelemetry_api) to the Key Dependencies table with purpose 'OpenTelemetry observability (traces, metrics, logs)'. Add a section documenting the --dart-define flags for configuring the OTLP endpoint.
Step 9 - Update .kiro/steering/structure.md: Add the new files (telemetry_service.dart, telemetry_config.dart) to the project structure listing under lib/services/.
acceptance_criteria: FlutterOTel.initialize() is called during app startup in main.dart with appropriate service name and version
Flutter errors and zone errors are reported to OpenTelemetry via FlutterOTel.reportError()
KiroApi HTTP calls are wrapped in OTel trace spans with http.method, operation name, and error recording
DebugLog optionally bridges log entries to the OTel backend when a TelemetryService is configured
TelemetryService follows the project's abstract interface + DI pattern and is provided via Provider
OTLP endpoint is configurable via --dart-define compile-time flags (OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT, etc.)
All existing tests continue to pass (KiroApi tests work without telemetry injected since it is optional)
New unit tests for TelemetryService and TelemetryConfig pass
Steering files (tech.md, structure.md) are updated to reflect the new dependency and files
verification: Run 'flutter analyze' and confirm no issues
Run 'flutter test' and confirm all tests pass (existing and new)
Verify lib/main.dart imports and initializes FlutterOTel
Verify lib/services/kiro_api.dart has span instrumentation on API methods
Verify test/services/telemetry_service_test.dart exists and tests pass
findings: All 9 steps implemented successfully. flutter analyze shows 10 issues, all pre-existing (3 errors in test/views/ due to FakeAuthManager missing handleWebSignInWithTokens, 3 warnings for unused elements, 4 infos for use_build_context_synchronously). flutter test: 41 passed, 3 failed (same pre-existing FakeAuthManager failures). 11 new telemetry tests all pass. TelemetryService uses dynamic tracer getter to avoid coupling the abstract interface to the concrete OTel SDK types. KiroApi _startSpan helper uses dynamic dispatch for nullable span operations. The toAttributes() extension from flutterrific_opentelemetry is used for creating Attributes from Map<String, Object>. FlutterOTel.initialize() is async so main.dart runZonedGuarded callback was made async.
